status: public reviewer handoff card for benchmark based safety wording

source_anchors.name: No Ranking Benchmark Misuse Warning issue
source_anchors.supports: public route for ten blocked benchmark misuse patterns

source_anchors.name: MedHELM
source_anchors.supports: benchmark task, workflow, metric, and leaderboard context

source_anchors.name: OpenAI HealthBench
source_anchors.supports: rubric based health evaluation and leakage control context

source_anchors.name: BRIDGE
source_anchors.supports: language, specialty, document type, care stage, and data boundary context

source_anchors.name: CHAI AI Governance
source_anchors.supports: governance lane framing for benchmark language review

handoff_steps.reviewer_question: what exact sentence will appear in public
handoff_steps.blocked_state: sentence is too vague to review

handoff_steps.reviewer_question: which benchmark metric or score is being mentioned

handoff_steps.step: Convert score language into task language
handoff_steps.reviewer_question: what task user care setting and workflow does the statement actually support
handoff_steps.blocked_state: score is still framed as general safety

handoff_steps.reviewer_question: does the sentence imply any blocked misuse claim
handoff_steps.blocked_state: any blocked claim is present or ambiguous

handoff_steps.reviewer_question: what evidence would a reader need before trusting the public wording
handoff_steps.blocked_state: evidence packet is missing and the sentence still sounds like a safety claim

handoff_steps.step: Protect examples and hidden test content
handoff_steps.reviewer_question: does the public text copy protected benchmark content
handoff_steps.blocked_state: benchmark example or answer content is exposed

handoff_steps.reviewer_question: does the public sentence tell the reader what the benchmark can and cannot show
handoff_steps.blocked_state: sentence still reads as ranking superiority or certified safety
